stories.title: Walker Webcast: Willy Walker Cuts Through the Market Noise
stories.excerpt: In the post-pandemic years of 2023 and 2024, many in commercial real estate believed that although the economy wasn’t great and there wasn’t much money to be made, “survive until ’25” was a mantra to live by.  “Unfortunately, we all survived to ’25 and then nothing had happened,” Walker & Dunlop chairman and CEO Willy Walker told the audience in his introductory remarks at the company’s Summer Conference.  Although the market is beginning to rebound a year later, the rate of progress isn’t aligning with anyone’s projections. “The saying now seems to be, ‘there will be a slice of heaven in ’27,’ ” Walker continued.   In the current environment, said Walker, it’s important to cut through the noise. “What is it that the data is actually telling us?” he said. “It feels as if every one of us has an Instagram feed that is filled with noise. It’s clickbait, it’s fun to look at, but it doesn’t really tell you anything.” Walker aimed to cut through some of that noise, noting that the employment rate has remained steady, belying fears of artificial intelligence taking away jobs. Nonetheless, public opposition to AI and data centers has persisted, with about $131 billion of data center projects turned down by local municipalities in the first quarter of 2026. “While AI is ready to go, the government is going to come in and it’s going to slow it down,” said Walker.  Another factor is how the U.S. is faring from a competitive standpoint against China when it comes to AI usage. The month-to-month growth the use of token is one metric: China more than doubled from 46 trillion tokens in May to 98 trillion in June, while th
bullets: Walker & Dunlop CEO: market recovery slower than projected, delayed to 2027.
Municipal opposition blocked ~$131B data center projects in Q1 2026.
Employment steady despite AI fears; regulatory headwinds slowing tech deployment.
stories.summary: Willy Walker, Walker & Dunlop chairman and CEO, warned that post-pandemic recovery has stalled—the "survive until '25" mantra has given way to expectations for relief in 2027. Employment remains stable, contradicting AI job-loss predictions. However, approximately $131 billion in data center projects faced municipal rejection in early 2026, as local governments slow AI infrastructure development despite industry readiness.
stories.impact: Regulatory delays on data center and AI infrastructure will dampen job creation in tech hubs (Austin, Northern Virginia, Denver) through 2027, pressuring near-term multifamily demand growth. Extended recovery timeline suggests continued cap-rate pressure and reduced development velocity nationwide.

stories.title: AI Firm Mercor Brings One World Trade to 97% Leased
stories.excerpt: The Durst Organization and The Port Authority of New York and New Jersey announced that Mercor has signed a lease at One World Trade Center for 25,550 square feet on the 77th floor. The term length was not disclosed. With this agreement, One World Trade Center is 97% leased and Mercor joins the growing community of artificial intelligence companies with offices at the 3.1-million-square-foot tower. “One World Trade Center was built for forward-looking, cutting-edge companies looking for a world-class home in New York City. We look forward to welcoming Mercor to the building,” said Jody Durst, president of The Durst Organization. The Durst Organization was represented in-house by Eric Engelhardt, Karen Rose, and Sayo Kamara and by Newmark’s David Falk, Peter Shimkin, Hal Stein, Nathan Kropp, and Paige Raisides. Harry Singer of CBRE represented Mercor. The post AI Firm Mercor Brings One World Trade to 97% Leased appeared first on Connect CRE.
bullets: Mercor (AI firm) leases 25,550 sq ft at One World Trade Center, 77th floor.
One World Trade now 97% leased; growing cluster of AI tenants in tower.
3.1M sq ft office tower near full occupancy signals tech/AI demand in NYC.
stories.summary: Mercor, an AI company, signed a lease for 25,550 square feet on the 77th floor of One World Trade Center, bringing the 3.1-million-square-foot tower to 97% occupancy. The Durst Organization and Port Authority announced the deal as the tower has become a hub for cutting-edge, forward-looking companies, particularly in artificial intelligence.
stories.impact: Strong office leasing in Midtown Manhattan (One World Trade location) by high-wage tech/AI firms signals sustained demand for premium residential in nearby FiDi and Flatiron submarkets, supporting luxury multifamily value and rent growth in Lower Manhattan.

stories.title: Historic Cinerama Dome, Adjacent 14-Screen Complex Set for 2028 Reopening
stories.excerpt: Shuttered since the pandemic, the historic Cinerama Dome in Hollywood and surrounding 14-screen movie theater are set to reopen under the management of Alamo Drafthouse Cinema, a unit of Sony Pictures Entertainment. CBRE’s Nicole Mihalka, along with Carl Muhlstein of Muhlstein CRE, and Scott Burns and Shauna Mattis of JLL jointly represented the ownership of the Dome Entertainment Center, while JLL’s Ken Shishido represented Alamo Drafthouse. Opened in 1963, the Dome will undergo restorative renovations starting next month through early 2028. Its historic legacy and singular moviegoing experience will be honored and carefully preserved, with the iconic “Cinerama” name and exterior branding left intact. The adjacent complex, formerly run by ArcLight Cinemas, will be operated as an Alamo Drafthouse dine-in cinema. “This lease marks the beginning of an exciting new chapter for one of Hollywood’s most iconic and beloved properties,” said Mihalka. “The Cinerama Dome has been a cultural landmark for generations, and bringing Sony Entertainment and Alamo Drafthouse to the Dome Entertainment Center is a strong vote of confidence in the future of theatrical entertainment and the continued demand for shared moviegoing experiences.” The post Historic Cinerama Dome, Adjacent 14-Screen Complex Set for 2028 Reopening appeared first on Connect CRE.
bullets: Cinerama Dome & 14-screen complex reopen under Alamo Drafthouse, Sony in 2028.
Historic theater shuttered since pandemic; $M+ restoration underway through early 2028.
Signals confidence in Hollywood entertainment district and shared-experience venues.
stories.summary: The Cinerama Dome and adjacent 14-screen ArcLight complex in Hollywood, closed since the pandemic, will reopen in early 2028 under Alamo Drafthouse (Sony Pictures Entertainment subsidiary). The iconic 1963 theater will undergo full restorative renovation beginning next month. The deal signals renewed confidence in theatrical exhibition and experiential entertainment venues.
stories.impact: Minimal direct impact on multifamily development. Reopening affirms Hollywood as a cultural/tourist anchor, which modestly supports nearby residential demand (Los Feliz, Hollywood Hills, Koreatown submarkets), but entertainment venues do not drive multifamily fundamentals; housing demand driven by employment and population.

stories.title: Roselle Park Apartments Trade After Brokers Resolve Permit Issue
stories.excerpt: Roselle Grande, a 119-unit garden-style apartment community located at 220-250 W. Sumner Ave. in Roselle Park, NJ, has changed hands for an undisclosed price, in a transaction arranged by SAGE Investment Real Estate Advisors. The firm’s brokerage team of partners and co-founders Steve Tragash and Greg Pine, alongside principal Eli Herskowitz and associate Katie Migner, exclusively represented the seller, Roselle Grande LLC, and procured the unnamed buyer. During the seller’s deliverables phase, the transaction encountered an unresolved permit issue. Recognizing the importance of a coordinated effort, SAGE spearheaded a resolution for which Tragash and Migner spent nearly a month consulting with and engaging engineering companies to provide the town’s construction department with required documentation.  “Today’s commercial real estate environment requires an engaged and knowledgeable advisor who is willing to step outside the traditional box,” said Tragash. Built in 1970 and situated on a parcel at the intersection of Roselle Park and Kenilworth, Roselle Grande consists of four buildings with a mix of studio, one-bedroom and two-bedroom layouts. The stabilized property features historically high occupancy rates, on-site parking and dedicated laundry facilities.  The conversations behind tomorrow’s apartment deals happen at Connect Apartments on September 22 in LA. Connect with the capital sources, owners, developers, and brokers driving multifamily investment. Learn more and reserve your seat at www.connectapts2026.com. The post Roselle Park Apartments Trade After Brokers Resolve Permit Issue appeared first on Connect CRE.
bullets: Roselle Grande, 119-unit garden complex in Roselle Park, NJ traded; price undisclosed.
Built 1970; stabilized asset with high occupancy; 4 buildings, mixed unit types.
Broker overcame permit issue during closing; illustrates operational complexity in transaction.
stories.summary: Roselle Grande, a 119-unit garden-style apartment community in Roselle Park, New Jersey, changed hands after SAGE Investment Real Estate Advisors resolved a permit issue during the seller's deliverables phase. The 1970-built property features four buildings with studio, one-, and two-bedroom units, maintains historically high occupancy, and includes on-site parking and laundry.
stories.impact: Limited market signal. Secondary-market New Jersey garden-apartment trades occur regularly; this note chiefly reflects that institutional capital continues to acquire stabilized garden-style assets in suburban markets, supporting values. No demand or supply implications for development pipelines.

stories.title: Jon Pezzoni Joins Hunneman’s Leasing & Advisory Team as SVP
stories.excerpt: Hunneman said Wednesday that Jon Pezzoni has joined the firm’s Leasing & Advisory team as an SVP and Principal. He brings more than a decade of experience across Greater Boston’s office, R&D and industrial markets. Pezzoni was most recently an SVP at Avison Young. Since 2014, Pezzoni has been advising tenants, landlords, and developers throughout the Metro West, 495 West & South submarkets, devising and executing strategies that best position assets to maximize their value. “Jon has built an outstanding reputation throughout the Metro West market by consistently delivering exceptional results for his clients,” said Steve Prozinski, CEO & COO of Hunneman. “His expertise across office, R&D, and industrial properties, combined with his collaborative approach and deep local relationships, make him an excellent addition to our Leasing & Advisory team. We’re excited to welcome Jon to Hunneman and look forward to the value he’ll bring to both our clients and our firm.” The post Jon Pezzoni Joins Hunneman’s Leasing & Advisory Team as SVP appeared first on Connect CRE.
bullets: Jon Pezzoni joins Hunneman as SVP after decade at Avison Young.
Focus on Greater Boston Metro West, 495 West & South industrial/office markets.
Senior hire signals active leasing/advisory expansion in Boston tech corridor.
stories.summary: Jon Pezzoni, an industry veteran with over a decade of experience advising tenants, landlords, and developers across Greater Boston's office, R&D, and industrial markets, has joined Hunneman as Senior Vice President and Principal. Pezzoni previously led efforts across Metro West, 495 West, and South Boston submarkets for Avison Young since 2014.
stories.impact: Minimal direct multifamily relevance. Senior hire in office/industrial advisory does not signal demand or supply shifts affecting residential development in Boston. Hire reflects broker consolidation and competition for tenant representation; multifamily developers should monitor competing capital flows and office-to-residential conversions if Boston office vacancy rises.

stories.title: Alonzo Mourning, HTG net $55M financing package for affordable housing
stories.excerpt: NBA hall-of-famer Alonzo Mourning and Housing Trust Group scored a $55.4 million financing package for a Naranja affordable housing complex. The deal comes as the duo has racked up financing in recent years for below-market rate rentals in Miami-Dade County.  Mourning, through his AM Affordable Housing, and Coconut Grove-based Housing Trust Group plan the 115-unit Artisan Pointe project at 26115 South Dixie Highway in an unincorporated area of south Miami-Dade County. The four-story complex is under construction with completion expected in January 2028, according to the developers’ news release.  The financing package includes $31.5 million in low-income housing tax credit […]This article originally appeared on The Real Deal. Click here to read the full story.
bullets: Alonzo Mourning & Housing Trust Group secure $55.4M for 115-unit Naranja affordable project.
Artisan Pointe: 30%-70% AMI-restricted; completion Jan 2028; South Miami-Dade County.
$31.5M LIHTC equity, $16.9M permanent loan (Berkadia), $7M CRA funding.
stories.summary: Alonzo Mourning's AM Affordable Housing and Housing Trust Group closed $55.4 million in financing for Artisan Pointe, a 115-unit affordable project in south Miami-Dade County (unincorporated Naranja area). The four-story complex, under construction with January 2028 completion, is capitalized with $31.5 million in LIHTC equity, a $16.9 million permanent loan arranged by Berkadia, and $7 million from the Naranja Lakes Community Redevelopment Agency. Units are restricted to 30%, 60%, and 70% AMI.
stories.impact: LIHTC-funded affordable supply entering Miami-Dade in 2028 will moderate rent pressure in south County and demonstrate strong public-subsidy appetite for workforce housing. Market-rate developers should monitor LIHTC pipeline in submarket (Naranja, Palmetto, West Kendall) to assess gap between affordable and market rents; competition for residents below 80% AMI intensifies with each LIHTC delivery.

stories.title: Meal Delivery Service CookUnity to Open Flagship Storefront at NoMad Hotel
stories.excerpt: CookUnity, a subscription-based meal delivery platform, has inked 4,721 square feet of ground-floor retail space at Lam Group’s 1227 Broadway, also known as Virgin Hotel New York City, in Manhattan’s NoMad neighborhood, Commercial Observer has learned. The deal represents the chef-prepared meal service company’s first flagship storefront in New York City, a source close to the deal told CO. The store is expected to open in the first half of 2027. CookUnity launched in 2015 and is headquartered in Brooklyn at 630 Flushing Avenue. The company also has an existing Manhattan kitchen hub at 534 West 50th Street in Hell’s Kitchen and a Flatiron District office at 36 Lexington Avenue. It’s unclear whether its new NoMad spot represents a fourth location or a relocation of an existing site. The asking rent and the length of the lease were also not available. The average asking rent for retail space in the nearby neighborhood around Herald Square was $394 per square foot during the second quarter of 2026, according to data from CBRE. The landlord was represented by Adam Weinblatt and Harley Bonn from Newmark, according to the source. CookUnity was represented by Alexandra Yanoff and Christine Nebiar from Brand Urban. Newmark declined to comment. “We’re excited to see CookUnity’s first flagship land at the Virgin Hotel in NoMad, a great signal for the brand’s evolution from delivery only to a space where people can finally interact with it in real life through fine dining and retail,” Nebiar said in a statement. CookUnity’s new flagship will feature dining and retail options, as well as space for community gatherings for lovers of the culinary arts. The company delivers in New York, Chicago, Los Angeles, Miami, Atlanta, Austin, Chicago, Seattle and Toronto. Virgin, a 38-story hotel tower on Broadway between West 29th and West 30th streets, opened in 2023 and provides guests with over 400 hotel rooms, a rooftop pool and restaurant Everdene, which o
bullets: CookUnity (meal delivery) leases 4,721 sq ft ground-floor retail at Virgin Hotel NoMad.
First flagship NYC storefront; opening H1 2027 at 1227 Broadway, Manhattan.
Signals strong retail recovery in NoMad; avg. asking rent ~$394/sq ft nearby.
stories.summary: CookUnity, a subscription-based meal delivery platform headquartered in Brooklyn, has leased 4,721 square feet of ground-floor retail space at 1227 Broadway (Virgin Hotel New York City) in Manhattan's NoMad neighborhood. The flagship storefront is expected to open in the first half of 2027. Average asking rent in the nearby Herald Square area was $394 per square foot in Q2 2026.
stories.impact: Minimal multifamily impact. Ground-floor retail leasing in NoMad reflects urban consumer confidence and foot traffic, which supports residential values in the NoMad/Flatiron neighborhoods, but retail tenancy does not drive housing supply or demand. Multifamily developers should note NoMad's continued vitality as a mixed-use neighborhood.

stories.title: Endeavor boosts Austin office pipeline with 5th and Walsh project
stories.excerpt: Endeavor’s 5th and Walsh project in Downtown Austin, which will also house its new headquarters, is finally getting off the ground.  Plans for the new five-story, 198,000-square-foot office mid-rise and retail building were submitted to the city in March 2022, more than four years ago. The development will feature 21,000 square feet of restaurant and retail space on the ground floor and a below-grade parking garage, according to the Austin Business Journal.  The project is expected to be completed in the later months of 2028, and Endeavor will promptly move into the building from its old headquarters at 500 West […]This article originally appeared on The Real Deal. Click here to read the full story.
bullets: Endeavor's 5th and Walsh (198K sq ft, 5-story office/retail) 50%+ preleased before groundbreak.
Endeavor HQ relocation from 500 W. 5th to 1200 W. 5th; completion late 2028.
Amazon exploring Dog's Head manufacturing campus (45-year agreement path); Endeavor owns land.
stories.summary: Endeavor's 5th and Walsh office project in Downtown Austin—a five-story, 198,000-square-foot building housing Endeavor's new headquarters plus 21,000 square feet of ground-floor retail—is 50% preleased before construction begins (late 2028 completion). Separately, Amazon is in negotiations to develop an advanced robotics manufacturing facility on Endeavor-owned Dog's Head property (2,614 acres), with the city approving annexation and opening the door to a 45-year development agreement.
stories.impact: Amazon manufacturing facility in Austin will attract high-wage engineering and operations jobs, driving substantial multifamily demand in Central Texas through 2029–2031. Endeavor's office preleasing signals office-market confidence in Downtown Austin despite broader office softness. Both projects suggest strong in-migration to Austin; developers should prioritize land acquisition and permitting in submarkets near Dog's Head (South Austin, Mueller, Riverside) and Downtown for delivery 2028–2032.

stories.title: It’s Finally Showtime Again at Hollywood’s Cinerama Dome
stories.excerpt: Get your popcorn ready, Los Angeles. One of Hollywood’s most beloved movie theaters is finally coming back. Sony Pictures Entertainment and its Alamo Drafthouse Cinema subsidiary announced Wednesday they struck a long-term lease to restore and reopen the Cinerama Dome on Sunset Boulevard and the adjacent 14-screen former ArcLight Cinemas complex, six years after both went dark during the pandemic. “Hot damn! We’re bringing the Dome back, baby,” Tom Rothman, chairman and CEO of Sony’s Motion Picture Group, said in a statement. “We believe in moviegoing down to our soul, and no venue on Earth stands more for that than the one-of-a-kind Dome. I am deeply proud that Sony is investing in Hollywood, literally.” The Cinerama Dome is expected to reopen in early 2028, with renovations beginning next month to preserve its 86-foot-wide curved screen and large-format presentation. JLL’s Ken Shishido represented Alamo Drafthouse in the lease. CBRE’s Nicole Mihalka, Muhlstein Commercial’s Carl Muhlstein and JLL’s Scott Burns and Shauna Mattis represented the ownership of the Dome Entertainment Center. Alamo will operate the landmark, but the Dome will not offer dine-in service like at typical Alamo theaters. The neighboring former ArcLight multiplex will reopen as an Alamo Drafthouse with dine-in service, 4K laser projection and 70-millimeter cinematic capabilities. Sony acquired Alamo Drafthouse in 2024. The deal carries far more significance in addition to filling a long-vacant theater complex. “This lease marks the beginning of an exciting new chapter for one of Hollywood’s most iconic and beloved properties,” Mihalka said in a statement. “The Cinerama Dome has been a cultural landmark for generations, and bringing Sony Entertainment and Alamo Drafthouse to the Dome Entertainment Center is a strong vote of confidence in the future of theatrical entertainment and the continued demand for shared moviegoing experiences.” The unmistakable landmark opened in 1963. It’s a Los
bullets: Cinerama Dome & 14-screen ArcLight reopen via Sony/Alamo Drafthouse in early 2028.
Six-year pandemic closure; long-term lease; restorations begin next month.
Sony investing in Hollywood theatrical recovery; 86-foot curved screen preserved.
stories.summary: Sony Pictures Entertainment and Alamo Drafthouse Cinema announced a long-term lease to restore and reopen the Cinerama Dome and adjacent 14-screen former ArcLight complex on Sunset Boulevard, six years after both closed during the pandemic. Renovations begin next month; reopening expected early 2028. Sony plans to preserve the iconic 86-foot curved screen and large-format presentation experience.
stories.impact: See idx 3. Reopening signals Hollywood's continued cultural standing and confidence in experiential entertainment, modestly supporting nearby residential neighborhoods (Los Feliz, Hollywood Hills), but does not materially drive multifamily supply or demand forecasting.

stories.title: Bascom Buys 183-Unit Orange County, Calif., Complex for $53M
stories.excerpt: The Bascom Group has acquired a 183-unit apartment complex in Orange County, Calif., as the company expands its multifamily holdings this year in the Western U.S. The Irvine, Calif.-based investment firm put down $53.1 million for Castlewood Park Apartments in Buena Park. The deal is equal to about $290,200 per unit. Institutional Property Advisors (IPA), a division of Marcus & Millichap, announced the deal after the brokerage’s Brian Eisendrath, Cameron Chalfant, Jesse Zarouk and Jake Vitta arranged the financing. IPA’s Kevin Green, Joe Grabiec and Gregory Harris represented the seller, which was not disclosed. Property records show Newcastle owned the property for more than 30 years. IPA described Buena Park as one of the most supply-constrained submarkets in Orange County. “Castlewood Park is the largest multifamily sale to transact in Buena Park and one of only eight $50 million-plus multifamily sales in north Orange County in the last 24 months,” Green said in a statement. Built in 1963, Castlewood Park includes 46 buildings across 8.7 acres at 6968 Campus Drive, adjacent to Buena Park High School. The community features of two-, three- and four-bedroom apartments averaging 1,028 square feet. It was renovated a few times from 2010 through 2023. Bascom plans to renovate apartment interiors, upgrade the property’s amenities, and add full-time on-site management. Bascom said it has acquired 13 apartment communities totaling 3,231 units for more than $930 million since federal interest rates started rising after the pandemic. Earlier this year, Bascom acquired a 294-unit property in Las Vegas and a 289-unit in Tucson, Ariz. Gregory Cornfield can be reached at gcornfield@commercialobserver.com.
bullets: Bascom Group buys Castlewood Park (183-unit, built 1963) in Buena Park, CA for $53.1M.
$290.2K per unit; Buena Park described as supply-constrained Orange County submarket.
Largest multifamily sale in Buena Park; 1 of 8 $50M+ sales in N. OC (24 mo.).
stories.summary: Bascom Group acquired Castlewood Park Apartments in Buena Park, Orange County, California, for $53.1 million ($290,200 per unit). The 183-unit complex, built in 1963, sits on 8.7 acres and includes 46 buildings. Institutional Property Advisors described Buena Park as one of Orange County's most supply-constrained multifamily submarkets; this is the largest multifamily transaction in Buena Park and one of only eight $50 million-plus sales in North Orange County in the past 24 months.
stories.impact: Supply scarcity in Buena Park and North OC is driving institutional capital to older, stabilized garden-complex acquisitions at rising per-unit prices. Scarcity signals limited new supply and sustained rent growth potential in the submarket; developers should prioritize entitled, shovel-ready projects in N. OC (Anaheim, Cypress, Garden Grove) for delivery 2028–2030 to capture pent-up demand.

stories.title: Starwood files data center plan as Virginia residents revolt
stories.excerpt: Barry Sternlicht’s Northern Virginia data center drive is forging ahead, even as residents plan to revolt against Starwood Capital Group’s dreams. Starwood filed a permit application for its two-building project at the 34-acre Plaza 500 on the Alexandria town line in Virginia, the Washington Business Journal reported. The first building, Plaza 500 South, is planned for more than 226,000 square feet, according to the pending application. HKS Inc. is the architect for the project. The first phase of construction is expected to carry a $5 million price tag, according to Fairfax County records. In addition to the two data center […]This article originally appeared on The Real Deal. Click here to read the full story.
bullets: Starwood Capital files data center permit for Plaza 500 (226K+ sq ft phase 1) in Alexandria, VA.
Proposed substation 100 feet from homes in Bren Mar; resident opposition mounted.
First phase carries ~$5M construction cost; residents plan September rally.
stories.summary: Barry Sternlicht's Starwood Capital Group filed a permit application for a two-building data center project at the 34-acre Plaza 500 on the Alexandria, Virginia town line, purchased four years ago for $165 million. The first building, Plaza 500 South, is planned for 226,000+ square feet; first-phase construction estimated at $5 million. A proposed electrical substation within 100 feet of homes in the Bren Mar neighborhood has sparked resident opposition and a planned rally during the September planning commission review.
stories.impact: Regulatory delay risk for Northern Virginia data center pipeline delays job creation (high-wage tech, AI, cloud infrastructure) in Loudoun/Fairfax through 2027–2028, reducing near-term multifamily demand inflow. However, Starwood's persistence and dual-site acquisition suggest eventual approval; developers should expect eventual data center anchors in Alexandria/Herndon corridor, supporting housing demand 2028+. Monitor local political opposition—residential NIMBYism against infrastructure may reshape Fairfax/Loudoun growth patterns.

stories.title: JP Morgan Finances Recap of Industrial Portfolio With $209M Loan
stories.excerpt: A joint venture between Centerbridge Partners and Henderson Group has landed a $208.5 million debt package to recapitalize 40 industrial assets in Pennsylvania and South Florida, Commercial Observer has learned. J.P. Morgan Chase supplied the five-year, floating-rate loan on the sponsorship’s logistics portfolio comprising 2.3 million square feet and featuring a mix of last-mile warehouses, distribution sites and shallow-bay facilities. The properties, which have buildings ranging from 16,000 to 155,000 square feet, are 95 percent leased to 110 tenants. CBRE negotiated the debt with a team consisting of Tom Traynor, Tom Rugg, Mark Finan and Henry Fenmore. J.P. Morgan, Centerbridge and Henderson Group did not immediately return requests for comment. CBRE declined to comment. Andrew Coen can be reached at acoen@commercialobserver.com.
bullets: J.P. Morgan provides $208.5M loan to Centerbridge/Henderson on 40-property industrial portfolio.
2.3M sq ft across Pennsylvania & South Florida; 95% leased, 110 tenants.
5-year floating rate; reflects institutional appetite for logistics recapitalization.
stories.summary: J.P. Morgan Chase provided a $208.5 million five-year, floating-rate loan to a joint venture between Centerbridge Partners and Henderson Group to recapitalize a 40-property industrial portfolio comprising 2.3 million square feet across Pennsylvania and South Florida. The portfolio is 95% leased to 110 tenants, featuring a mix of last-mile warehouses, distribution sites, and shallow-bay facilities with buildings ranging from 16,000 to 155,000 square feet. CBRE negotiated the debt.
stories.impact: Minimal direct multifamily impact. Industrial refinancing reflects stable logistics demand and lender confidence in last-mile real estate; however, capital flowing to industrial recaps does not compete directly with multifamily development financing. Suggests robust debt availability for core-plus/value-add assets nationally; multifamily sponsors with stabilized portfolios should expect competitive loan terms.

stories.title: Vlad Doronin joining forces with South Korea juggernaut
stories.excerpt: Vlad Doronin’s global conquest for the Aman brand is taking the company to South Korea and beyond. Doronin’s OKO Group and the Shinsegae Group, one of the top retail conglomerates in the Asian nation, formed a fund to develop luxury hotels and more, Bloomberg reported. The fund is being launched with an initial $500 million investment. The partners are kicking things off with a mixed-use project in Seoul’s Gangnam District, which will include an Aman hotel, residences and a club, according to Doronin. As they continue to fundraise, the partners will expand out into more of Asia, the Middle East […]This article originally appeared on The Real Deal. Click here to read the full story.
bullets: Vlad Doronin (OKO) partners with Shinsegae Group (South Korea); $500M initial investment.
Aman mixed-use Seoul Gangnam project (hotel, residences, club) launches partnership.
Aman sold $9B in branded residences in 4 years; 20 hotels under construction.
stories.summary: Vlad Doronin's OKO Group and the Shinsegae Group, a South Korean retail conglomerate, formed a fund with an initial $500 million investment to develop luxury hotels, residences, and clubs across Asia, the Middle East, and the United States. The partners are launching with a mixed-use project in Seoul's Gangnam District. Aman has sold $9 billion in branded residences over four years and has approximately 20 hotels under construction globally.
stories.impact: Negligible direct impact on U.S. multifamily development markets. International luxury residential (Aman residences) targets ultra-high-net-worth buyers, not core rental demand. However, signals that Doronin and OKO are securing large capital commitments for luxury branded residential; U.S. luxury apartment developers in gateway cities (NYC, LA, Miami) may see increased competition from international branded-residence platforms.

stories.title: Grubb Properties forms $1.9B apartment REIT after recapitalization
stories.excerpt: Grubb Properties wants a bigger balance sheet and bigger financing, forming a multifamily real estate investment trust to that end. The Charlotte-based developer formed a REIT by consolidating several of its funds, Bisnow reported. Link Apartments REIT, which will not be traded or opened up for additional investment, counts 45 properties and more than 5,600 units as part of its portfolio, valued at approximately $1.9 billion. Perhaps the most notable property in the portfolio is 8 Carlisle, a rental tower under construction in Manhattan’s Financial District. This month, Grubb secured a $377 million construction loan for its 462-unit rental project. […]This article originally appeared on The Real Deal. Click here to read the full story.
bullets: Grubb Properties forms Link Apartments REIT; 45 properties, 5,600+ units, ~$1.9B valuation.
Includes 8 Carlisle (462-unit FiDi rental tower); $377M construction loan secured this month.
Grubb secured $617M total financing; REIT enables larger balance sheet for future growth.
stories.summary: Grubb Properties formed Link Apartments REIT by consolidating multiple funds, creating a non-traded trust valued at approximately $1.9 billion with 45 properties and 5,600+ units. The portfolio includes 8 Carlisle, a 462-unit rental tower under construction in Manhattan's Financial District. This month, Grubb secured a $377 million construction loan for 8 Carlisle (comprising a $300 million loan from Maxim Capital and a $77 million mezzanine from Skylight), plus a $240 million credit facility. Total financing across transactions reached $617 million.
stories.impact: Large publicly valued multifamily REIT formation signals robust capital availability for high-quality rental assets and construction financing in gateway markets (especially Manhattan). 8 Carlisle's $377M financing (low leverage for prime FiDi location) reflects continued investor confidence in NYC luxury multifamily despite office weakness. Grubb's REIT structure enables future acquisitions and development; developers should expect Grubb as an active acquirer of value-add and development-stage assets in Northeast gateway markets.

stories.title: CDK Lending Supplies $37M Loan for Newark Apartments Project
stories.excerpt: RBH Group has landed $36.7 million of pre-development financing for a mixed-use multifamily project in Newark, N.J., Commercial Observer has learned. CDK Lending provided the loan on the developer’s planned 420-unit apartment complex with ground-floor retail space at 101 Market Street in Newark’s Four Corners neighborhood near downtown. The debt pays off a previous loan provided by Goldman Sachs for the long-planned development, which is located within a 5.65-acre land assemblage site. Meridian Capital Group arranged the transaction with a team led by David Bollag and Naphtali Marrus. “Newark’s downtown has seen remarkable momentum over the past decade, and a development-ready assemblage of this scale in the central business district is exceptionally hard to come by,” Bollag said in a statement. RBH Group has been assembling land for a mixed-use development consisting of 49 separate contiguous lots since 2005, according to Bollag. The firm also plans to commence a 241-unit multifamily project at nearby 193 Market Street next year and is eyeing other developments at the site roughly half a mile from Newark Penn Station. Newark-based RBH Group is also developing the Teachers Village project at 243 Halsey Street in Newark featuring 203 apartments geared toward local educators. CDK Lending and RBH Group did not immediately return requests for comment. Andrew Coen can be reached at acoen@commercialobserver.com.
bullets: CDK Lending provides $36.7M pre-development financing for RBH's 420-unit Newark project.
101 Market Street, Four Corners neighborhood; ground-floor retail; 5.65-acre assemblage.
Replaces Goldman Sachs prior loan; Meridian Capital arranged; closing imminent.
stories.summary: CDK Lending provided $36.7 million in pre-development financing for RBH Group's mixed-use multifamily project at 101 Market Street in Newark, New Jersey's Four Corners neighborhood. The planned 420-unit apartment complex with ground-floor retail sits on a 5.65-acre land assemblage RBH has been assembling since 2005 (49 separate contiguous lots). The debt replaces a prior Goldman Sachs loan; Meridian Capital Group arranged the transaction.
stories.impact: Newark Four Corners/downtown shows sustained institutional capital commitment for large-scale multifamily development. 420-unit project on 5.65-acre site signals continued belief in Newark's downtown revitalization and regional demand from NYC-adjacent spillover. Financing availability (Goldman Sachs → CDK Lending) indicates competitive debt market for pre-development stage; Newark developers should accelerate entitled projects to market before financing tightens. Expect delivery 2027–2029.

stories.title: NYC’s top construction permits: Week ending July 17, 2026
stories.excerpt: 🏆 New Building: The largest new building application filed last week was on the border of Crown Heights and Prospect-Lefferts Gardens. Rafael Rabinowitz of HSD Construction plans to build a 112,279-square-foot, eight-story development at 103 Empire Boulevard. The mixed-use project is slated to have 50 apartments. Kao Hwa Lee is the architect. Job Filing Number: B01408304-I1 📊 New Building: Borough Developers filed plans to construct an 82,731-square-foot mixed-use project at 45-51 and 45-49 Vernon Boulevard in Queens. The building will stand 10 stories tall and have 86 apartments. Kao Hwa Lee is the architect. Job Filing Numbers: Q01425664-I1, Q01425663-I1 📊 […]This article originally appeared on The Real Deal. Click here to read the full story.
bullets: NYC permits: 112-unit mixed-use (Crown Heights/Prospect-Lefferts), 86-unit Queens, 198-unit Bronx complex.
Week of July 17, 2026; multiple mid-rise residential projects filed across all boroughs.
Reflects ongoing NYC multifamily development pipeline despite office sector headwinds.
stories.summary: Top NYC construction permits filed the week ending July 17, 2026 include: (1) 112-unit mixed-use development at 103 Empire Boulevard (Crown Heights/Prospect-Lefferts border, 8 stories); (2) 86-unit, 10-story mixed-use project at 45-51 and 45-49 Vernon Boulevard in Queens; (3) two 99-unit, nine-story apartment buildings in the Bronx (761 East Tremont and 1962 Prospect). Major alterations also filed at Brookfield's 250 Vestry Street (Financial District) and JPMorgan Chase renovation of 126 Myrtle Avenue (Downtown Brooklyn).
stories.impact: Steady residential pipeline across NYC's outer boroughs (Queens, Bronx) and secondary Manhattan neighborhoods (Crown Heights, Brooklyn) signals sustained housing demand outside Midtown office markets. Projects in transit-accessible outer-borough locations indicate developer confidence in affordability/workforce housing demand. Entitlement velocity suggests deliveries 2028–2030; monitor zoning variances and financing availability as leading indicators of project viability.
